title: Contract Tests, Race and Leak Checks, Chaos, and Release · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for contract tests, race and leak checks, chaos, and release and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a production Go messaging defense dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Inject return, nack, channel close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, stale generation, race, and goroutine-leak failures.

Roll out schema, topology, publisher, consumer, policy, and broker changes in a compatibility-safe order with shadow evidence and rollback; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Layered Test Strategy · changed case · Quiz Layered Test Strategy
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 3713 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate release on contracts, race and leak evidence, broker faults, compatibility, rollback, and upgrade path; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
